title: Profitability: Margin × Turnover = ROA
event: Ortiz Co. reports net income of $45,000 on net sales of $500,000. Average total assets were $400,000. Compute profit margin, total asset turnover, and return on assets.

computationPaths.narration: ROA decomposes: how much of each sales dollar you keep (margin) times how hard each asset dollar works (turnover).

memorize.body: ROA = Profit margin × Asset turnover = (NI ÷ Sales) × (Sales ÷ Avg assets).
Sales cancels in the middle — so ROA is also just NI ÷ average assets. Two roads, same answer: use one to check the other.

distractors.feedback: That's ROA — income against ASSETS. Margin divides by SALES: 45 ÷ 500 = 9%.
That's asset turnover, a times-multiple, not a percent of sales.
